title: Stop Giving Gumroad 10%% on Every PDF Sale - There Is a Smarter Way
content:
You have PDFs sitting on your hard drive right now that people would pay for. Study guides, business templates, design checklists, meal prep plans, technical docs. PDFs are the simplest digital product to create and sell - zero production cost, universal format, instant delivery.

But here is where most creators mess up: they list on a marketplace that skims 10-20%% of every sale. On 100 sales of a $25 PDF, that is $250-$500/month going to a middleman. Over a year, you are handing over $3,000-$6,000 for... what exactly? A listing page?

## What PDFs Actually Sell in 2026

Not all PDFs are worth selling. The ones that convert consistently solve a specific problem:

- **Educational guides** - Certification prep, software tutorials, skill-based how-tos. Highest-performing category across all platforms.
- **Business templates** - Invoice formats, project proposals, pitch decks. Freelancers buy these on repeat.
- **Creative resources** - Font pairing guides, brand style templates, social media calendars.
- **Technical documentation** - API guides, configuration manuals, troubleshooting handbooks. Developers pay premium for well-organized technical content.
- **Printables** - Planners, trackers, checklists, wall art. Popular on Etsy but you lose 15%% in fees there.

## The Creation Workflow

1. **Write** in Google Docs or Notion. Tight 15-page guide > padded 50-page one.
2. **Design** in Canva (free tier works). Consistent fonts, colors, callout boxes.
3. **Create a cover page** that looks professional - this is your listing thumbnail.
4. **Proofread** with Grammarly. Typos destroy credibility in paid products.
5. **Export as PDF**, test on mobile and desktop.

## Pricing That Works

- Simple checklists/templates: $5-$15
- Comprehensive guides (20-50 pages): $15-$40
- Premium resources with updates: $30-$75

Pro tip: Offer a free 5-10 page sample, then sell the full version. Builds trust and gets people into your store.

## The Platform Decision

Here is the real question: where do you list?

**Marketplaces (Etsy, Gumroad)** - Easy but 5-20%% fees. You do not own the customer relationship.

**Self-hosted (WooCommerce)** - Full control but $20-30/month before your first sale. Plugin headaches.

**Direct payment platforms** - Branded store, automated delivery, buyers pay YOU directly.

[3DIMLI](https://www.3dimli.com/) is in that third category. 0%% commission. Payments go straight to your PayPal, Stripe, or Razorpay. Your store, your customers, your money.

## Setup Takes 10 Minutes

1. [Register as a seller](https://www.3dimli.com/register) (free during beta)
2. Brand your store - name, banner, bio, social links
3. Connect PayPal/Stripe/Razorpay in Settings
4. Upload PDF as Ebook product type (up to 1024MB)
5. Set license-based pricing - Standard $15 personal, Commercial $40 for business use
6. Add preview images (cover page, sample pages, table of contents)

## The Math That Matters

100 sales/month at $25 per PDF:
- 10%% fee platform: you lose $250/month = $3,000/year
- 20%% fee platform: you lose $500/month = $6,000/year  
- 0%% commission: you keep everything (standard payment processing fees still apply)

## Scaling Beyond One PDF

- **Bundle related PDFs** together at a discount using [product variants](https://support.3dimli.com/Guides/creating-product-variants)
- **Bulk upload** with [Watch Folder](https://support.3dimli.com/Guides/bulk-uploads%%20and%%20watch%%20folder) when you have many products
- **Enable Pay-What-You-Want** on some products to maximize reach
- **Branch out** - 3DIMLI supports 9 product types including Graphics, Audio, Software, Video, and AI Models

---

Your PDFs are worth money. Stop letting platforms skim the top. [Start your free store on 3DIMLI](https://www.3dimli.com/register) - 0%% commission, direct payments, 10 minutes to set up.
